norwegian: Da vi kom fram, hadde toget allerede gått.
english: When we arrived, the train had already left.

norwegian: Jeg hadde aldri smakt rakfisk før jeg besøkte Fagernes.
english: I had never tasted rakfisk before I visited Fagernes.

norwegian: Hun hadde _____ (sove) i to timer da alarmen ringte.
english: She had slept for two hours when the alarm rang.

norwegian: De hadde glemt å bestille bord, så de måtte vente.
english: They had forgotten to book a table, so they had to wait.

norwegian: Han hadde _____ (jobbe) der i ti år da han sluttet.
english: He had worked there for ten years when he quit.

norwegian: Før eksamen hadde vi lest gjennom alle notatene.
english: Before the exam, we had read through all the notes.

norwegian: Jeg skjønte at jeg hadde _____ (ta) feil buss.
english: I realized I had taken the wrong bus.

norwegian: Vi ble lei oss fordi vi hadde mistet billettene.
english: We were upset because we had lost the tickets.

norwegian: Da jeg ringte, hadde hun allerede _____ (legge seg).
english: When I called, she had already gone to bed.

norwegian: Han fortalte at han hadde bodd i Bergen i fem år.
english: He said that he had lived in Bergen for five years.

norwegian: Filmen hadde allerede _____ (begynne) da vi kom inn.
english: The film had already started when we came in.

norwegian: Jeg hadde ikke forventet at jobben ville være så krevende.
english: I had not expected the job to be so demanding.

norwegian: De hadde _____ (spise) middag da gjestene ankom.
english: They had eaten dinner when the guests arrived.

norwegian: Hun hadde aldri vært i utlandet før hun fikk jobb i Paris.
english: She had never been abroad before she got a job in Paris.

norwegian: Jeg visste ikke at du hadde _____ (flytte) til Oslo.
english: I didn't know you had moved to Oslo.

norwegian: Hadde dere spist før dere dro?
english: Had you eaten before you left?

norwegian: Vi oppdaget at noen hadde _____ (ta) syklene våre.
english: We discovered that someone had taken our bikes.

norwegian: Innen vi kom hjem, hadde det sluttet å regne.
english: By the time we got home, it had stopped raining.

norwegian: Han hadde _____ (skrive) tre rapporter den uken.
english: He had written three reports that week.

norwegian: Læreren hadde allerede rettet prøvene da vi kom tilbake fra pause.
english: The teacher had already marked the tests when we returned from break.

norwegian: Jeg husket plutselig at jeg hadde glemt nøklene hjemme.
english: I suddenly remembered that I had forgotten the keys at home.

norwegian: Da møtet begynte, hadde halvparten av deltakerne _____ (gå) hjem.
english: When the meeting started, half the participants had gone home.

norwegian: Barna hadde aldri sett så mye snø.
english: The children had never seen so much snow.

norwegian: Jeg hadde _____ (lese) halvparten av boken da strømmen gikk.
english: I had read half the book when the power went out.

norwegian: Turister hadde strømmet til byen lenge før festivalen startet.
english: Tourists had been streaming into the city long before the festival started.

norwegian: hadde / hun / laget / allerede / mat / da / kom / han
english: She had already made food when he arrived.
notes: Hun hadde allerede laget mat da han kom.

norwegian: De hadde _____ (kjøpe) huset for lenge siden.
english: They had bought the house a long time ago.

norwegian: Jeg / hadde / aldri / prøvd / brunost / før
english: I had never tried brunost before.
notes: Jeg hadde aldri prøvd brunost før.

norwegian: Vi hadde snakket om det mange ganger, men aldri gjort noe med det.
english: We had talked about it many times, but never done anything about it.

norwegian: allerede / hadde / de / da / landet / vi / ankom / flyet
english: The plane had already landed when we arrived.
notes: Flyet hadde allerede landet da vi ankom.

norwegian: Han sitter og leser avisen hver morgen.
english: He sits reading the newspaper every morning.

norwegian: Vi så en _____ (løpe) hund i parken.
english: We saw a running dog in the park.

norwegian: Barnet lå og gråt i senga si.
english: The child lay crying in its bed.

norwegian: Det er en _____ (stige) tendens til at folk jobber hjemmefra.
english: There is a rising tendency for people to work from home.

norwegian: Hun sto og ventet på bussen i regnet.
english: She stood waiting for the bus in the rain.

norwegian: De satt og spilte kort hele kvelden.
english: They sat playing cards all evening.

norwegian: Det er en _____ (bekymre) situasjon for mange familier.
english: It is a worrying situation for many families.

norwegian: Jeg lå og tenkte på det lenge.
english: I lay thinking about it for a long time.

norwegian: Han gikk og mumlet noe for seg selv.
english: He walked along muttering something to himself.

norwegian: Det var en _____ (vokse) kø utenfor butikken.
english: There was a growing queue outside the shop.

norwegian: Vi satt og pratet i timevis.
english: We sat chatting for hours.

norwegian: Det er et _____ (overraske) resultat fra valget.
english: It is a surprising result from the election.

norwegian: Hun sto og stirret ut av vinduet.
english: She stood staring out of the window.

norwegian: Det er et _____ (øke) problem med plastforurensning.
english: There is an increasing problem with plastic pollution.

norwegian: Barna lå og leste tegneserier på gulvet.
english: The children lay reading comics on the floor.

norwegian: Det kom en _____ (smile) dame mot oss.
english: A smiling lady came towards us.

norwegian: Han sitter og jobber sent hver kveld.
english: He sits working late every evening.

norwegian: Det var en _____ (forvirre) beskjed fra sjefen.
english: It was a confusing message from the boss.

norwegian: Vi stod og så på fjordene fra dekket.
english: We stood looking at the fjords from the deck.

norwegian: Det er et _____ (love) tegn at han begynte å øve mer.
english: It is a promising sign that he started practising more.

norwegian: Jeg gikk og tenkte på det hele veien hjem.
english: I walked thinking about it the whole way home.

norwegian: Han / satt / og / spiste / frokost / alene
english: He sat eating breakfast alone.
notes: Han satt og spiste frokost alene.

norwegian: Det var en _____ (synke) følelse i magen.
english: There was a sinking feeling in the stomach.

norwegian: hun / lå / og / hørte / på / musikk / i / sengen
english: She lay listening to music in bed.
notes: Hun lå og hørte på musikk i sengen.

norwegian: Vi / satt / og / så / på / TV / hele / ettermiddagen
english: We sat watching TV all afternoon.
notes: Vi satt og så på TV hele ettermiddagen.

norwegian: Det var et _____ (engasjere) foredrag om klimaet.
english: It was an engaging lecture about the climate.

norwegian: Han sto og ventet utenfor døren i fem minutter.
english: He stood waiting outside the door for five minutes.

norwegian: Det var et _____ (utvikle) kurs som lærte meg mye.
english: It was an enriching course that taught me a lot.

norwegian: De gikk og handlet mens barna lå og sov hjemme.
english: They went shopping while the children lay sleeping at home.

norwegian: Det er Kari som har gjort det.
english: It is Kari who did it.

norwegian: Det er _____ (han/ham) som betaler regningen.
english: It is he who pays the bill.

norwegian: Det var i Bergen at han vokste opp.
english: It was in Bergen that he grew up.

norwegian: Det er maten _____ gjør restauranten spesiell.
english: It is the food that makes the restaurant special.

norwegian: Det er i januar at det er kaldest her.
english: It is in January that it is coldest here.

norwegian: Det er læreren _____ har ansvaret for klassen.
english: It is the teacher who is responsible for the class.

norwegian: Det var sønnen hennes som vant konkurransen.
english: It was her son who won the competition.

norwegian: Det er om sommeren _____ vi pleier å dra til hytta.
english: It is in summer that we usually go to the cabin.

norwegian: Det er treningen som gjør deg sterkere.
english: It is the training that makes you stronger.

norwegian: Det var ikke pengene _____ motiverte henne, men selve arbeidet.
english: It was not the money that motivated her, but the work itself.

norwegian: Det er han som vet best hva som skjedde.
english: It is he who knows best what happened.

norwegian: Det er på fredag _____ møtet holdes.
english: It is on Friday that the meeting is held.

norwegian: Det var her vi møttes for første gang.
english: It was here that we met for the first time.

norwegian: Det er barna _____ lider mest av konflikten.
english: It is the children who suffer most from the conflict.

norwegian: Det var kaffen som holdt meg våken under møtet.
english: It was the coffee that kept me awake during the meeting.

norwegian: Det / er / norsk / jeg / lærer / som / studerer
english: It is Norwegian that I am studying.

norwegian: Det er naturen _____ tiltrekker flest turister til Norge.
english: It is the nature that attracts most tourists to Norway.

norwegian: Det var i går at hun fikk nyheten.
english: It was yesterday that she got the news.

norwegian: Det er tidspress _____ gjør prosjektet vanskelig.
english: It is time pressure that makes the project difficult.

norwegian: Det var gjennom venner at jeg hørte om jobben.
english: It was through friends that I heard about the job.

norwegian: Det / var / moren / sin / som / han / ringte / til
english: It was his mother that he called.
notes: Det var moren sin han ringte til.

norwegian: Det er ikke lett _____ man forstår det uten hjelp.
english: It is not easy to understand it without help.

norwegian: Det er hunden som er familiens beste venn.
english: It is the dog that is the family's best friend.

norwegian: Det var på jobben _____ han møtte kona si.
english: It was at work that he met his wife.

norwegian: Det er sommeren _____ vi husker best fra barndommen.
english: It is the summers that we remember most from childhood.

norwegian: Det / er / på / lørdag / at / vi / reiser
english: It is on Saturday that we are travelling.
notes: Det er på lørdag at vi reiser.

norwegian: Det er ikke jeg som bestemmer det.
english: It is not I who decide that.

norwegian: Det var fisken _____ gjorde middagen så god.
english: It was the fish that made the dinner so good.

norwegian: Det er nettopp dette at jeg prøvde å forklare deg.
english: It is precisely this that I was trying to explain to you.

norwegian: Det / var / vennen / hans / som / ga / ham / rådet
english: It was his friend who gave him the advice.
notes: Det var vennen hans som ga ham rådet.

norwegian: Butikken åpnes klokka ni hver dag.
english: The shop opens at nine every day.

norwegian: Broen ble _____ (bygge) i 1970.
english: The bridge was built in 1970.

norwegian: Mange filmer selges på nettet i dag.
english: Many films are sold online today.

norwegian: Bilen ble _____ (stjele) natt til mandag.
english: The car was stolen on Sunday night.

norwegian: Det snakkes mye om klimaendringene.
english: There is a lot of talk about climate change.

norwegian: Pengene ble _____ (finne) i en gammel koffert.
english: The money was found in an old suitcase.

norwegian: Det serveres frokost mellom sju og ti.
english: Breakfast is served between seven and ten.

norwegian: Sykehuset ble _____ (åpne) av kongen i fjor.
english: The hospital was opened by the king last year.

norwegian: Post leveres ikke på søndager.
english: Post is not delivered on Sundays.

norwegian: Rapporten ble _____ (skrive) av tre forskere.
english: The report was written by three researchers.

norwegian: Norsk tales av omtrent fem millioner mennesker.
english: Norwegian is spoken by approximately five million people.

norwegian: Mannen ble _____ (arrestere) av politiet.
english: The man was arrested by the police.

norwegian: Dørene låses automatisk klokka seks.
english: The doors lock automatically at six.

norwegian: Huset ble _____ (male) i sommer.
english: The house was painted this summer.

norwegian: Mange jobber tapes på grunn av automatisering.
english: Many jobs are being lost due to automation.

norwegian: Boken ble _____ (gi) ut av et lite forlag.
english: The book was published by a small publisher.

norwegian: Søknaden behandles innen to uker.
english: The application will be processed within two weeks.

norwegian: Møtet ble _____ (avlyse) på kort varsel.
english: The meeting was cancelled at short notice.

norwegian: Varene sendes innen tre virkedager.
english: The goods are sent within three working days.

norwegian: Premien ble _____ (dele) ut av ordføreren.
english: The prize was handed out by the mayor.

norwegian: Det selges ferske grønnsaker på torget.
english: Fresh vegetables are sold at the market.

norwegian: Skogen / ble / hogget / ned / av / et / selskap
english: The forest was felled by a company.
notes: Skogen ble hogget ned av et selskap.

norwegian: Resultater publiseres på nettsiden etter hvert.
english: Results are published on the website gradually.

norwegian: Filmen ble _____ (lage) med et lite budsjett.
english: The film was made with a small budget.

norwegian: Alle passasjerer bes om å ha pass klart.
english: All passengers are asked to have their passport ready.

norwegian: Veien / ble / stengt / på / grunn av / flom
english: The road was closed due to flooding.
notes: Veien ble stengt på grunn av flom.

norwegian: Det bes om at passasjerene venter ved gate B.
english: Passengers are asked to wait at gate B.

norwegian: Avtalen ble _____ (inngå) mellom de to landene.
english: The agreement was entered into between the two countries.

norwegian: Bøker lånes ut gratis på biblioteket.
english: Books are lent out for free at the library.

norwegian: Kandidaten / ble / valgt / av / et / flertall
english: The candidate was elected by a majority.
notes: Kandidaten ble valgt av et flertall.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske jeg hadde mer tid til å lese.
english: I wish I had more time to read.

norwegian: Det ville vært fint om du kunne komme på lørdag.
english: It would be nice if you could come on Saturday.

norwegian: Om jeg bare _____ (ha) mer penger, ville jeg reist til Japan.
english: If only I had more money, I would have travelled to Japan.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske det ikke regnet så mye her.
english: I wish it didn't rain so much here.

norwegian: Det ville vært bedre om vi hadde bestilt bord på forhånd.
english: It would have been better if we had booked a table in advance.

norwegian: Om jeg bare _____ (vite) svaret, ville jeg fortalt deg.
english: If only I knew the answer, I would have told you.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske jeg kunne snakke norsk flytende.
english: I wish I could speak Norwegian fluently.

norwegian: Det ville vært fint om vi hadde _____ (bo) nærmere hverandre.
english: It would be nice if we lived closer to each other.

norwegian: Om jeg bare hadde tatt paraplyen med meg.
english: If only I had taken my umbrella with me.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske sjefen min var mer forståelsesfull.
english: I wish my boss was more understanding.

norwegian: Det ville vært _____ (godt) om vi fikk litt varmere vær.
english: It would be great if we got a bit warmer weather.

norwegian: Om jeg bare hadde startet å trene tidligere.
english: If only I had started exercising earlier.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske vi _____ (ha) en større leilighet.
english: I wish we had a bigger flat.

norwegian: Det ville vært enklere om vi hadde planlagt dette bedre.
english: It would have been easier if we had planned this better.

norwegian: Om jeg bare _____ (kunne) ta fri denne uken.
english: If only I could take the week off.

norwegian: skulle / jeg / ønske / at / vi / bodde / på / landet
english: I wish we lived in the countryside.
notes: Jeg skulle ønske at vi bodde på landet.

norwegian: Det ville vært fint om du _____ (hjelpe) meg med dette.
english: It would be nice if you could help me with this.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske at barn fikk mer tid til lek.
english: I wish children had more time for play.

norwegian: ville / det / vært / fint / om / komme / du / kunne / tidlig
english: It would be nice if you could come early.
notes: Det ville vært fint om du kunne komme tidlig.

norwegian: Om vi bare hadde _____ (se) værmeldingen, hadde vi blitt hjemme.
english: If only we had seen the weather forecast, we would have stayed home.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske jeg hadde lært å spille gitar da jeg var ung.
english: I wish I had learnt to play guitar when I was young.

norwegian: Det ville vært bedre om _____ (ta) toget i stedet for bilen.
english: It would have been better to take the train instead of the car.

norwegian: Om jeg bare hadde sovet mer i natt.
english: If only I had slept more last night.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske maten her ikke var så dyr.
english: I wish the food here wasn't so expensive.

norwegian: om / bare / jeg / hadde / spurt / om / hjelp / tidligere
english: If only I had asked for help earlier.
notes: Om jeg bare hadde spurt om hjelp tidligere.

norwegian: Det ville vært fint om vi _____ (få) sol i helgen.
english: It would be nice if we got sunshine over the weekend.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske jobben lå nærmere hjemmet mitt.
english: I wish work was closer to my home.

norwegian: Om jeg bare hadde _____ (huske) å sende e-posten.
english: If only I had remembered to send the email.

norwegian: Det ville vært fint om alle kunne møtes til jul.
english: It would be nice if everyone could meet up for Christmas.

norwegian: Jeg skulle ønske jeg hadde valgt et annet studie.
english: I wish I had chosen a different degree.

norwegian: Det vet du jo at vi alltid spiser middag klokka fem.
english: You know, of course, that we always eat dinner at five.

norwegian: Han er _____ (jo/nok) hjemme nå — lyset er på.
english: He is probably home now — the light is on.

norwegian: Det er vel ikke så farlig om vi kommer litt sent?
english: It's probably not such a big deal if we arrive a bit late?

norwegian: Du kjenner _____ (jo/vel) Oslo ganske godt fra før?
english: You know Oslo quite well already, don't you?

norwegian: Altså, jeg forstår ikke hvorfor de ikke svarte.
english: So, I don't understand why they didn't reply.

norwegian: Det er _____ (da/jo) klart at du må si fra hvis noe er galt.
english: It's clear, of course, that you must speak up if something is wrong.

norwegian: Været er liksom ikke akkurat det vi hadde håpet på.
english: The weather is sort of not exactly what we had hoped for.

norwegian: Hun snakker _____ (nok/vel) fem språk flytende.
english: She probably speaks five languages fluently.

norwegian: Jeg vet jo at det er vanskelig, men vi må prøve.
english: I know, of course, that it is difficult, but we have to try.

norwegian: Det er _____ (vel/da) ikke verdt å stresse over det?
english: It's probably not worth stressing about that?

norwegian: Altså, du kunne jo ha sagt fra om det.
english: So, you could have told me about that, you know.

norwegian: Vi tar _____ (da/nok) en pause etter dette møtet.
english: We'll take a break after this meeting, then.

norwegian: Det er jo slik det fungerer her i landet.
english: That's just how things work in this country, you know.

norwegian: Hun er _____ (vel/jo) omtrent tjue år gammel, tror jeg.
english: She's probably about twenty years old, I think.

norwegian: Det er liksom vanskelig å forklare hva jeg mener.
english: It's sort of hard to explain what I mean.

norwegian: jo / du / at / vet / bergen / er / kjent / for / regnet
english: You know, of course, that Bergen is famous for the rain.
notes: Du vet jo at Bergen er kjent for regnet.

norwegian: Vi må _____ (da/nok) finne en løsning på dette.
english: We'll have to find a solution to this, then.

norwegian: Altså, jeg synes det er litt for mye lekser.
english: I mean, I think there is a bit too much homework.

norwegian: Det er _____ (nok/jo) best å ringe dem før vi drar.
english: It's probably best to call them before we leave.

norwegian: Han er liksom alltid sen til møter.
english: He's sort of always late to meetings.

norwegian: det / er / jo / ikke / gratis / dette
english: This isn't free, you know.
notes: Det er jo ikke gratis, dette.

norwegian: Vi tar _____ (vel/nok) en kopp kaffe til?
english: We'll have one more cup of coffee, I suppose?

norwegian: Du vet jo at jeg ikke liker når du gjør det.
english: You know very well that I don't like it when you do that.

norwegian: Altså, det her er _____ (da/jo) ikke normalt.
english: I mean, this is really not normal.

norwegian: Det er nok ikke så enkelt som det ser ut.
english: It's probably not as simple as it looks.

norwegian: altså / jeg / ikke / skjønner / det / her
english: I just don't get this.
notes: Altså, jeg skjønner ikke det her.

norwegian: Det er _____ (vel/jo) normalt at man er nervøs før en presentasjon.
english: It's quite normal, you know, to be nervous before a presentation.

norwegian: Vi tar det da med ro og ser hva som skjer.
english: We'll just take it easy and see what happens.

norwegian: Han er liksom _____ (vel/nok) den flinkeste på laget.
english: He's sort of probably the most skilled on the team.

norwegian: Altså, det er da klart at vi må finne en bedre løsning.
english: So, it's clear that we need to find a better solution.

norwegian: Jeg ga opp å lære kinesisk etter tre måneder.
english: I gave up trying to learn Chinese after three months.

norwegian: Vi fant _____ (ut/opp) at toget var kansellert.
english: We found out that the train was cancelled.

norwegian: Han tok av seg jakken og satte seg ned.
english: He took off his jacket and sat down.

norwegian: Kan du stå _____ (opp/på) litt tidligere i morgen?
english: Can you get up a bit earlier tomorrow?

norwegian: De slo seg ned i en liten by utenfor Stavanger.
english: They settled down in a small town outside Stavanger.

norwegian: Hun kom _____ (tilbake/inn) fra ferie med mye energi.
english: She came back from holiday with lots of energy.

norwegian: Jeg finner ikke ut av denne oppgaven.
english: I can't figure out this task.

norwegian: Barna tok _____ (av/på) seg skoene ved døren.
english: The children took their shoes off at the door.

norwegian: Han ga ikke opp selv om det var vanskelig.
english: He didn't give up even though it was difficult.

norwegian: Vi kom _____ (tilbake/frem) til hotellet sent på kvelden.
english: We came back to the hotel late in the evening.

norwegian: Hun stod opp klokka seks og jogget en runde.
english: She got up at six and went for a jog.

norwegian: Jeg fant _____ (ut/opp) at han hadde bodd i Tromsø.
english: I found out that he had lived in Tromsø.

norwegian: De slo seg ned i Oslo etter å ha bodd i utlandet.
english: They settled in Oslo after having lived abroad.

norwegian: Han tok _____ (av seg/på seg) genseren fordi det ble for varmt.
english: He took off his jumper because it got too warm.

norwegian: Vi måtte gi opp planen på grunn av dårlig vær.
english: We had to give up the plan because of bad weather.

norwegian: hun / kom / tilbake / fra / biblioteket / med / tre / bøker
english: She came back from the library with three books.
notes: Hun kom tilbake fra biblioteket med tre bøker.

norwegian: De bestemte seg for å slå seg _____ (ned/opp) på landet.
english: They decided to settle in the countryside.

norwegian: Jeg stod opp for å hjelpe den eldre damen på bussen.
english: I stood up to help the elderly lady on the bus.

norwegian: Vi fant til slutt _____ (ut/av) hvem som hadde sendt pakken.
english: We finally found out who had sent the parcel.

norwegian: Han tok av seg hatten og hilste pent.
english: He took off his hat and greeted politely.

norwegian: ga / hun / til slutt / opp / håpet / om / å / vinne
english: She finally gave up hope of winning.
notes: Hun ga til slutt opp håpet om å vinne.

norwegian: Kan du _____ (komme tilbake/gi opp) hit i morgen?
english: Can you come back here tomorrow?

norwegian: De fant ut at prisen hadde gått opp med ti prosent.
english: They found out that the price had risen by ten percent.

norwegian: Hun stod _____ (opp/av) tidlig for å rekke det første toget.
english: She got up early to catch the first train.

norwegian: Ikke gi opp — du er nesten i mål.
english: Don't give up — you're almost at the finish line.

norwegian: de / slo / seg / ned / i / en / leilighet / nær / sentrum
english: They settled in a flat near the city centre.
notes: De slo seg ned i en leilighet nær sentrum.

norwegian: Han tok av seg brillene og gned seg i øynene.
english: He took off his glasses and rubbed his eyes.

norwegian: Flyvertinnen ba passasjerene om å _____ (sette seg ned/stå opp).
english: The flight attendant asked passengers to sit down.

norwegian: Vi kom tilbake til hytta trett men fornøyde.
english: We came back to the cabin tired but happy.

norwegian: jeg / fant / ut / at / bussen / ikke / gikk / på / søndag
english: I found out that the bus didn't run on Sundays.
notes: Jeg fant ut at bussen ikke gikk på søndag.

norwegian: Den store bilen utenfor er vår.
english: The big car outside is ours.

norwegian: Jeg vil gjerne ha _____ (gammel) brødet som er til overs.
english: I would like the old bread that is left over.

norwegian: Det kalde vannet fra fjellet smaker godt.
english: The cold water from the mountain tastes good.

norwegian: Kan du hente _____ (rød) jakken min fra gangen?
english: Can you fetch my red jacket from the hall?

norwegian: Den nye leiligheten deres er mye større.
english: Their new flat is much bigger.

norwegian: Jeg liker _____ (grønn) salaten best.
english: I like the green salad best.

norwegian: De gamle husene i sentrum har mye sjarm.
english: The old houses in the city centre have a lot of charm.

norwegian: Har du sett _____ (blå) pennen min?
english: Have you seen my blue pen?

norwegian: Den høye mannen i køen er broren min.
english: The tall man in the queue is my brother.

norwegian: Vi overnatter på _____ (liten) hotellet ved sjøen.
english: We are staying at the small hotel by the sea.

norwegian: Det lange bordet i stuen er kjøpt på en auksjon.
english: The long table in the living room was bought at an auction.

norwegian: Jeg vil gjerne prøve _____ (norsk) osten dere anbefaler.
english: I would like to try the Norwegian cheese you recommend.

norwegian: Den travle dagen på jobb tappet ham for energi.
english: The busy day at work drained him of energy.

norwegian: Barna liker best _____ (søt) desserten vi lager til jul.
english: The children like the sweet dessert we make for Christmas best.

norwegian: Det store fjordet nord for Bergen er vakkert om sommeren.
english: The large fjord north of Bergen is beautiful in summer.

norwegian: den / varme / suppen / smaker / godt / på / kalde / dager
english: The warm soup tastes good on cold days.
notes: Den varme suppen smaker godt på kalde dager.

norwegian: Jeg husker _____ (første) gangen jeg smakte brunost.
english: I remember the first time I tasted brown cheese.

norwegian: De nye studentene på kurset virker veldig motiverte.
english: The new students on the course seem very motivated.

norwegian: Han likte ikke _____ (hard) madrassen på hotellet.
english: He didn't like the hard mattress at the hotel.

norwegian: Den sterke vinden i går veltet sykkelen min.
english: The strong wind yesterday knocked over my bicycle.

norwegian: det / gamle / trehuset / ved / elven / er / fremdeles / vakkert
english: The old wooden house by the river is still beautiful.
notes: Det gamle trehuset ved elven er fremdeles vakkert.

norwegian: Kan du sende meg _____ (fersk) rapporten?
english: Can you send me the fresh report?

norwegian: De unge mennene på laget trente hardt.
english: The young men on the team trained hard.

norwegian: Vi bestilte _____ (dyr) rommet fordi det hadde utsikt.
english: We booked the expensive room because it had a view.

norwegian: Den friske luften på fjellet gjør meg glad.
english: The fresh air on the mountain makes me happy.

norwegian: de / nye / naboene / i / blokken / er / veldig / hyggelige
english: The new neighbours in the building are very friendly.
notes: De nye naboene i blokken er veldig hyggelige.

norwegian: Jeg vil gjerne ha _____ (varm) sjokoladen med krem.
english: I would like the warm chocolate with cream.

norwegian: Den mørke skogen bak hytta ser litt skummel ut om natten.
english: The dark forest behind the cabin looks a bit scary at night.

norwegian: Vi tok _____ (siste) plassene på toget.
english: We took the last seats on the train.

norwegian: Det kalde klimaet i nord tiltrekker seg mange turister om vinteren.
english: The cold climate in the north attracts many tourists in winter.

norwegian: Selv om det regnet, bestemte vi oss for å gå tur.
english: Even though it was raining, we decided to go for a walk.

norwegian: Jeg vet ikke om hun kommer, men hvis hun gjør det, blir vi veldig glade.
english: I don't know if she is coming, but if she does, we will be very happy.

norwegian: Han sa at han ikke visste _____ (når/om) bussen gikk.
english: He said he didn't know when the bus left.

norwegian: Fordi hun hadde glemt lommeboken hjemme, måtte han betale for henne.
english: Because she had forgotten her wallet at home, he had to pay for her.

norwegian: Selv om jeg er trøtt, _____ (klarer/vil) jeg å fullføre arbeidet.
english: Even though I am tired, I will manage to finish the work.

norwegian: Dersom du øver hver dag, vil du bli mye bedre.
english: If you practise every day, you will get much better.

norwegian: Han ble overrasket da han hørte at kollegaen _____ (slutte) jobben.
english: He was surprised when he heard that his colleague had quit the job.

norwegian: Siden toget var forsinket, rakk vi ikke middagen.
english: Since the train was delayed, we didn't make it to dinner.

norwegian: Læreren forklarte at det var viktig at alle _____ (møte) opp i tide.
english: The teacher explained that it was important that everyone turned up on time.

norwegian: Jeg ringte til henne fordi jeg var bekymret, men hun svarte ikke.
english: I called her because I was worried, but she didn't answer.

norwegian: selv / om / det / var / dyrt / kjøpte / han / billettene
english: Even though it was expensive, he bought the tickets.
notes: Selv om det var dyrt, kjøpte han billettene.

norwegian: Hun jobbet hardt slik at hun _____ (kunne) ta fri i jula.
english: She worked hard so that she could take time off at Christmas.

norwegian: Mens barna sov, laget foreldrene julaften-middag.
english: While the children slept, the parents made the Christmas Eve dinner.

norwegian: Vi ble enige om at vi skulle dra til hytta, _____ (men/selv om) ikke alle var med.
english: We agreed to go to the cabin, even though not everyone was coming.

norwegian: Dersom det ikke snør, kan vi ikke gå på ski i morgen.
english: If it doesn't snow, we can't go skiing tomorrow.

norwegian: fordi / han / ikke / hadde / spist / ble / han / svimmel
english: Because he hadn't eaten, he felt dizzy.
notes: Fordi han ikke hadde spist, ble han svimmel.

norwegian: Når jeg er ferdig med eksamen, _____ (planlegge) jeg en lang ferie.
english: When I am done with the exam, I am planning a long holiday.

norwegian: Han spurte om vi visste at fergen ikke gikk på mandag.
english: He asked if we knew that the ferry didn't run on Mondays.

norwegian: Siden vi ikke hadde bil, _____ (måtte) vi ta bussen begge veier.
english: Since we didn't have a car, we had to take the bus both ways.

norwegian: Selv om hun var nervøs, holdt hun en flott presentasjon.
english: Even though she was nervous, she gave an excellent presentation.

norwegian: mens / hun / ventet / leste / hun / en / bok
english: While she waited, she read a book.
notes: Mens hun ventet, leste hun en bok.

norwegian: Han trengte mer tid fordi oppgaven _____ (vise seg) å være vanskeligere enn han trodde.
english: He needed more time because the task turned out to be harder than he thought.

norwegian: Slik at alle forstår, snakker hun sakte og tydelig.
english: So that everyone understands, she speaks slowly and clearly.

norwegian: Vi skjønte ikke hva som hadde _____ (skje) før vi så nyhetene.
english: We didn't understand what had happened until we watched the news.

norwegian: Innen du er ferdig med å lese den, vil du forstå mye mer.
english: By the time you finish reading it, you will understand much more.

norwegian: dersom / du / ikke / melder / deg / på / mister / du / plassen
english: If you don't sign up, you will lose your place.
notes: Dersom du ikke melder deg på, mister du plassen.

norwegian: Hun bestemte seg for å ta eksamen selv om hun ikke _____ (føle) seg klar.
english: She decided to take the exam even though she didn't feel ready.

norwegian: Fordi hun ikke kjente byen, spurte hun en forbipasserende om veien.
english: Because she didn't know the city, she asked a passer-by for directions.

norwegian: Vi spiste ute siden det var _____ (fint) vær og restauranten hadde uteservering.
english: We ate out since the weather was nice and the restaurant had outdoor seating.

norwegian: Han forklarte at han ikke kunne komme fordi han var syk, men at han ville prøve å delta på neste møte.
english: He explained that he couldn't come because he was ill, but that he would try to attend the next meeting.

norwegian: Søknaden sendes per e-post til personalavdelingen.
english: The application is sent by email to the HR department.

norwegian: Kan _____ (De/du) vennligst fylle ut skjemaet?
english: Could you kindly fill in the form? (formal)

norwegian: Med vennlig hilsen, Kari Olsen.
english: Yours sincerely, Kari Olsen.

norwegian: Vi ber _____ (Dem/deg) om å bekrefte avtalen skriftlig.
english: We ask you to confirm the agreement in writing. (formal)

norwegian: Det bes om at alle passasjerer er ombord senest ti minutter før avgang.
english: All passengers are requested to be on board at least ten minutes before departure.

norwegian: Vedrørende _____ (din/Deres) forespørsel av 3. mai:
english: Regarding your enquiry of 3 May: (formal)

norwegian: Jeg skriver for å søke på stillingen som markedskoordinator.
english: I am writing to apply for the position of marketing coordinator.

norwegian: Kunden bes om å _____ (oppgi/si) referansenummeret ved henvendelse.
english: Customers are requested to provide the reference number when enquiring.

norwegian: Takk for hyggelig samvær — vi snakkes snart!
english: Thanks for a lovely time — talk soon!

norwegian: Søknadsfrist er _____ (den/sett) 15. juni.
english: The application deadline is 15 June.

norwegian: Vi tillater oss å minne om at fakturaen forfaller 30. mai.
english: We take the liberty of reminding you that the invoice falls due on 30 May.

norwegian: Har _____ (De/du) anledning til å møte oss på mandag klokka ti?
english: Are you available to meet us on Monday at ten? (formal)

norwegian: Hei Marte! Er du ledig på fredag kveld?
english: Hi Marte! Are you free on Friday evening?

norwegian: Kjære _____ (Herr/Hei) Andersen, vi bekrefter mottak av Deres brev.
english: Dear Mr Andersen, we confirm receipt of your letter.

norwegian: Det vil bli servert kaffe og kake etter møtet.
english: Coffee and cake will be served after the meeting.

norwegian: med / vennlig / hilsen / og / takk / for / samarbeidet
english: Yours sincerely and thank you for the cooperation.
notes: Med vennlig hilsen og takk for samarbeidet.

norwegian: Vi ber _____ (Dem/dere) om å ta kontakt dersom noe er uklart.
english: We ask you to get in touch if anything is unclear. (formal)

norwegian: Ansatte bes om å registrere seg i resepsjonen ved ankomst.
english: Employees are asked to register at reception on arrival.

norwegian: Vi ser frem til _____ (å høre/høre) fra Dem.
english: We look forward to hearing from you. (formal)

norwegian: Bistanden ytes i henhold til gjeldende regelverk.
english: Assistance is provided in accordance with current regulations.

norwegian: vi / ser / frem / til / å / møte / Dem / på / mandag
english: We look forward to meeting you on Monday. (formal)
notes: Vi ser frem til å møte Dem på mandag.

norwegian: Tilbudet gjelder _____ (til/inntil) og med 31. desember.
english: The offer is valid up to and including 31 December.

norwegian: Kjære alle, her er agendaen for møtet i morgen.
english: Dear all, here is the agenda for tomorrow's meeting.

norwegian: Vær _____ (vennlig/hyggelig) å besvare innen fredag.
english: Please reply by Friday. (formal written)

norwegian: Dokumentene fremlegges i original ved oppmøte.
english: Documents are to be presented in original at attendance.

norwegian: kunden / bes / om / å / oppgi / navn / og / adresse
english: The customer is asked to provide their name and address.
notes: Kunden bes om å oppgi navn og adresse.

norwegian: Vi ønsker _____ (Dem/dere) velkommen til årskonferansen.
english: We welcome you to the annual conference. (formal)

norwegian: Fant du boken du lette etter?
english: Did you find the book you were looking for?

norwegian: Besøkende bes om å _____ (skrive/si) seg inn i gjestelisten ved inngangen.
english: Visitors are requested to sign themselves into the guest list at the entrance.

norwegian: Vi håper å høre fra Dem og ser frem til videre samarbeid.
english: We hope to hear from you and look forward to further cooperation.

norwegian: Ha det bra, og lykke til med eksamen!
english: Goodbye, and good luck with the exam!

norwegian: Ta det _____ (med ro/alvorlig) — det er ikke krise.
english: Take it easy — it's not a crisis.

norwegian: Jeg har lyst til å prøve den nye restauranten i sentrum.
english: I feel like trying the new restaurant in the city centre.

norwegian: Han stod _____ (på/opp) selv om alle andre ga opp.
english: He kept at it even though everyone else gave up.

norwegian: Slå deg ned her — vi har god tid.
english: Settle in here — we have plenty of time.

norwegian: Har du _____ (lyst til/tid til) å gå på kino i kveld?
english: Do you feel like going to the cinema tonight?

norwegian: Hun stod på og fikk til slutt jobben hun drømte om.
english: She persevered and finally got the job she had dreamed of.

norwegian: Vi har det _____ (travelt/bra) for øyeblikket — det er mye å gjøre.
english: We are very busy at the moment — there is a lot to do.

norwegian: Ta det med ro — vi rekker toget.
english: Take it easy — we'll catch the train.

norwegian: Jeg har ikke _____ (lyst til/lov til) å spise mer kake.
english: I don't feel like eating more cake.

norwegian: Barna hadde det kjempegøy på stranden.
english: The children had a great time at the beach.

norwegian: Han _____ (stod på/ga opp) helt til han nådde toppen.
english: He kept at it until he reached the summit.

norwegian: Slå deg ned og fortell meg hva som skjedde.
english: Settle down and tell me what happened.

norwegian: Vi _____ (ha det bra/ha lyst) mens vi kan.
english: We'll enjoy ourselves while we can.

norwegian: Jeg har lyst til å lære å strikke denne vinteren.
english: I feel like learning to knit this winter.

norwegian: hun / stod / på / og / ble / til slutt / lege
english: She persevered and eventually became a doctor.
notes: Hun stod på og ble til slutt lege.

norwegian: Jeg har det _____ (bra/ille) takk — og du?
english: I'm fine, thanks — and you?

norwegian: Ta det med ro og ikke stress over dette.
english: Take it easy and don't stress about this.

norwegian: Har du _____ (lyst til/tenkt å) ta deg en ferie snart?
english: Do you feel like taking a holiday soon?

norwegian: Vi har det veldig travelt denne uken med alle møtene.
english: We are very busy this week with all the meetings.

norwegian: ta / det / med / ro / og / spis / litt / mer
english: Take it easy and eat a bit more.
notes: Ta det med ro og spis litt mer.

norwegian: Jeg _____ (har lyst til/er glad for) å danse, men er ikke så flink.
english: I feel like dancing, but I'm not very good.

norwegian: Han hadde det gøy på festen og møtte mange nye folk.
english: He had fun at the party and met many new people.

norwegian: Det er ikke alltid lett å _____ (stå på/ta det med ro) når man er stresset.
english: It's not always easy to keep going when you are stressed.

norwegian: Slå deg ned og ta deg en kopp te.
english: Settle down and have a cup of tea.

norwegian: jeg / har / det / bra / nå / etter / en / lang / hvil
english: I feel great now after a long rest.
notes: Jeg har det bra nå etter en lang hvil.

norwegian: Har du lyst _____ (på/til) litt mer mat?
english: Do you feel like a bit more food?

norwegian: Vi stod på og fullførte prosjektet i tide.
english: We persevered and finished the project on time.

norwegian: Barna _____ (hadde det bra/stod på) på skolen i dag.
english: The children had a good time at school today.

norwegian: Ha det bra på turen — send oss bilder!
english: Have a great trip — send us pictures!

norwegian: Han spurte om vi hadde spist allerede.
english: He asked if we had already eaten.

norwegian: Jeg lurer på _____ (om/hva) toget er forsinket.
english: I wonder if the train is delayed.

norwegian: Vet du hvor stasjonen er?
english: Do you know where the station is?

norwegian: Kan du si meg _____ (hva/om) bussen koster?
english: Can you tell me how much the bus costs?

norwegian: Hun lurte på om det var plass til dem på hotellet.
english: She wondered if there was room for them at the hotel.

norwegian: Jeg vet ikke _____ (hvem/hva) som skal lede møtet.
english: I don't know who is going to chair the meeting.

norwegian: De spurte om vi ville bli med på tur.
english: They asked if we wanted to join the trip.

norwegian: Kan du fortelle meg _____ (når/om) butikken stenger?
english: Can you tell me when the shop closes?

norwegian: Jeg lurer på hvorfor han ikke svarte på meldingen.
english: I wonder why he didn't reply to the message.

norwegian: Hun spurte _____ (om/hvor) det var langt til sentrum.
english: She asked if it was far to the city centre.

norwegian: Vet du hva klokka er?
english: Do you know what the time is?

norwegian: Jeg er ikke sikker på _____ (om/hva) han snakker sant.
english: I'm not sure whether he is telling the truth.

norwegian: Han visste ikke hvor lenge han hadde sovet.
english: He didn't know how long he had been sleeping.

norwegian: Kan du forklare meg _____ (hvordan/om) man bruker dette apparatet?
english: Can you explain to me how to use this device?

norwegian: De lurte på om prisen inkluderte frokost.
english: They wondered if the price included breakfast.

norwegian: jeg / lurer / på / om / hun / er / hjemme / nå
english: I wonder if she is home now.
notes: Jeg lurer på om hun er hjemme nå.

norwegian: Jeg spurte ham _____ (hva/om) han jobbet med.
english: I asked him what he worked with.

norwegian: Vet du om det er noen gode restauranter i nærheten?
english: Do you know if there are any good restaurants nearby?

norwegian: Han fortalte ikke _____ (hvem/om) han hadde snakket med.
english: He didn't say who he had spoken to.

norwegian: Jeg lurer på hvordan man sier det på norsk.
english: I wonder how you say that in Norwegian.

norwegian: kan / du / si / meg / hvor / neste / buss / går
english: Can you tell me when the next bus leaves?
notes: Kan du si meg hvor neste buss går?

norwegian: Læreren spurte _____ (om/hva) alle hadde forstått oppgaven.
english: The teacher asked whether everyone had understood the task.

norwegian: Jeg vet ikke hva jeg skal si til ham.
english: I don't know what to say to him.

norwegian: Hun lurte på _____ (hvorfor/hva) flyet var forsinket.
english: She wondered why the plane was delayed.

norwegian: Han spurte om vi hadde sett passet hans.
english: He asked if we had seen his passport.

norwegian: vet / du / om / det / er / ledig / rom / på / hotellet
english: Do you know if there are any rooms available at the hotel?
notes: Vet du om det er ledig rom på hotellet?

norwegian: Jeg spurte _____ (om/når) hun kunne komme.
english: I asked when she could come.

norwegian: Vi vet ikke hvem som betalte for middagen.
english: We don't know who paid for the dinner.

norwegian: Kan du si meg _____ (hvor/om) langt det er til grensen?
english: Can you tell me how far it is to the border?

norwegian: Jeg lurer på om det er mulig å bytte sete på flyet.
english: I wonder if it's possible to change seats on the plane.

norwegian: Jeg hadde spist middagen før jeg gikk til sengs
english: I had eaten dinner before I went to bed

norwegian: Jeg hadde spist frokost da jeg kom hjem
english: I had eaten breakfast when I came home

norwegian: Hun hadde gått en tur før hun dro til jobb
english: She had gone for a walk before she went to work

norwegian: Jeg hadde lest en bok før jeg dro til butikken
english: I had read a book before I went to the store

norwegian: De hadde dratt på ferie før de begynte å jobbe igjen
english: They had gone on vacation before they started working again

norwegian: Jeg hadde studert i flere timer da jeg tok eksamen
english: I had studied for several hours when I took the exam

norwegian: Jeg hadde tatt en tur før jeg dro til treningsstudioet
english: I had taken a walk before I went to the gym

norwegian: De hadde gjort husarbeid da de kom hjem fra jobb
english: They had done their chores when they came home from work

norwegian: Jeg hadde studert i flere timer før jeg tok eksamen
english: I had studied for several hours before I took the exam

norwegian: Jeg har vært i Norge i tre år, men er fortsatt ikke flytende.
english: I have been in Norway for three years but am still not fluent.

norwegian: Kan du legge fram planene dine på møtet i morgen?
english: Can you present your plans at the meeting tomorrow?

norwegian: Sjefen la fram budsjettet for styret i forrige uke.
english: The manager presented the budget to the board last week.

norwegian: Han slo opp ordet i ordboken.
english: He looked up the word in the dictionary.

norwegian: Hva holder du på med for tiden?
english: What are you up to these days?

norwegian: Jeg holdt på med leksene da telefonen ringte.
english: I was busy with the homework when the phone rang.

norwegian: Vi satte i gang prosjektet tidligere enn planlagt.
english: We started the project earlier than planned.

norwegian: De satte i gang med renoveringen i vår.
english: They got started on the renovation this spring.

norwegian: Legen ba meg ta opp problemet med kostholdet.
english: The doctor asked me to bring up the problem with my diet.

norwegian: Vi tok opp problemet med sjefen vår på fredag.
english: We raised the problem with our boss on Friday.

norwegian: Du kaster bort tid på å bekymre deg for det.
english: You are wasting time worrying about it.

norwegian: Det lønner seg ikke å kaste bort sjansen.
english: It doesn't pay to throw away the opportunity.

norwegian: Vi ryddet opp i leiligheten før gjestene kom.
english: We tidied up the flat before the guests arrived.

norwegian: Husker du å følge opp avtalen vi gjorde?
english: Do you remember to follow up on the agreement we made?

norwegian: Studenten fulgte opp forelesningen med ekstra lesing.
english: The student followed up the lecture with extra reading.

norwegian: Det er viktig å si fra hvis noe er galt på jobben.
english: It is important to speak up if something is wrong at work.

norwegian: De dro av gårde tidlig for å unngå rushtrafikken.
english: They set off early to avoid rush hour.

norwegian: Sykepleieren tok seg av pasienten hele natten.
english: The nurse looked after the patient all night.

norwegian: Han tok seg av katten da vi var bortreist.
english: He looked after the cat while we were away.

norwegian: Klarte du å komme over skuffelsen etter tapet?
english: Did you manage to get over the disappointment after the loss?

norwegian: Hun kom over en gammel venn på Grünerløkka.
english: She ran into an old friend in Grünerløkka.

norwegian: De trakk seg fra avtalen i siste liten.
english: They pulled out of the agreement at the last minute.

norwegian: Kan du henge med på tur i helgen?
english: Can you come along on the trip this weekend?

norwegian: De la ned mye arbeid i det nye produktet.
english: They put a lot of work into the new product.

norwegian: Han slo opp med kjæresten sin etter ferien.
english: He broke up with his girlfriend after the holiday.

norwegian: Barnet tok igjen de andre etter et par uker.
english: The child caught up with the others after a couple of weeks.

norwegian: De brøt av forhandlingene etter mange timer.
english: They broke off the negotiations after many hours.

norwegian: Han kom løpende inn i rommet.
english: He came running into the room.

norwegian: Barnet kom gående mot oss med et stort smil.
english: The child came walking towards us with a big smile.

norwegian: Hun kom syklende til jobben hver dag.
english: She came cycling to work every day.

norwegian: Han kom haltende inn etter fotballkampen.
english: He came limping in after the football match.

norwegian: Jeg ble sittende på kontoret til langt på kveld.
english: I ended up sitting at the office until late in the evening.

norwegian: Hun ble liggende etter at alarmen ringte.
english: She stayed lying down after the alarm went off.

norwegian: Turisten ble stående foran maleriet i lang tid.
english: The tourist remained standing in front of the painting for a long time.

norwegian: Han ble gående i timesvis uten å finne hotellet.
english: He kept walking for hours without finding the hotel.

norwegian: Vi hørte en syngende stemme fra naborommet.
english: We heard a singing voice from the next room.

norwegian: Det var en imponerende utsikt fra toppen av fjellet.
english: It was an impressive view from the top of the mountain.

norwegian: Han snakket med en smilende resepsjonist på hotellet.
english: He spoke with a smiling receptionist at the hotel.

norwegian: Det er en voksende interesse for norsk mat i utlandet.
english: There is a growing interest in Norwegian food abroad.

norwegian: Det var en utfordrende oppgave, men vi klarte det.
english: It was a challenging task, but we managed it.

norwegian: Det var et spennende møte med mange gode ideer.
english: It was an exciting meeting with many good ideas.

norwegian: Det var en overraskende nyhet for hele familien.
english: It was surprising news for the whole family.

norwegian: Det er en tidkrevende prosess å lære et nytt språk.
english: Learning a new language is a time-consuming process.

norwegian: Legen forklarte det på en beroligende måte.
english: The doctor explained it in a reassuring way.

norwegian: Det er et krevende yrke, men veldig givende.
english: It is a demanding profession, but very rewarding.

norwegian: Det var et avslørende svar som overrasket alle.
english: It was a revealing answer that surprised everyone.

norwegian: Det var en avslappende ferie ved sjøen.
english: It was a relaxing holiday by the sea.

norwegian: Han gikk syngende langs elven på vei hjem.
english: He walked along the river singing on his way home.

norwegian: Han stoppet en forbipasserende og spurte om veien.
english: He stopped a passer-by and asked for directions.

norwegian: Jeg slo to fluer i en smekk da jeg handlet og møtte sjefen.
english: I killed two birds with one stone when I shopped and ran into my boss.

norwegian: Hun tok beina på nakken da regnet begynte.
english: She ran off as fast as she could when the rain started.

norwegian: Det var helt Texas på kontoret i dag.
english: It was total chaos at the office today.

norwegian: Han har alltid en finger med i spillet på jobben.
english: He always has a hand in things at work.

norwegian: Det gikk som smurt på møtet i dag.
english: Everything went smoothly at the meeting today.

norwegian: Nå er katten ute av sekken.
english: The secret is out now.

norwegian: Jeg er helt på bærtur i dag — jeg glemmer alt.
english: I'm totally all over the place today — I keep forgetting everything.

norwegian: Han kastet kortene da oppgaven ble for vanskelig.
english: He gave up when the task became too difficult.

norwegian: Vi er på bølgelengde, så samarbeidet går bra.
english: We're on the same wavelength, so the collaboration is going well.

norwegian: Hun tok oksen ved hornene og løste problemet med en gang.
english: She took the bull by the horns and solved the problem right away.

norwegian: Jeg tror han har noe på hjertet som han ikke sier.
english: I think he has something on his mind that he's not saying.

norwegian: La oss ikke skyte spurv med kanoner her.
english: Let's not use a sledgehammer to crack a nut here.

norwegian: Vi snakket i vei hele kvelden uten å kjede oss.
english: We talked non-stop all evening without getting bored.

norwegian: Det er lenge siden vi sist møttes — tiden flyr.
english: It's been a long time since we last met — time flies.

norwegian: Hun holdt kortene tett til brystet under forhandlingene.
english: She played her cards close to her chest during the negotiations.

norwegian: Det er bare å bite i det sure eplet og gjøre ferdig rapporten.
english: You just have to bite the bullet and finish the report.

norwegian: Han satte fingeren på problemet med en gang.
english: He put his finger on the problem straight away.

norwegian: Det er ingen vits i å gråte over spilt melk.
english: There's no point crying over spilt milk.

norwegian: Etter ferien var jeg blakk — pengene gikk som vann.
english: After the holiday I was broke — the money went like water.

norwegian: Han er ikke akkurat noen lyspære, men han jobber hardt.
english: He's not exactly the sharpest tool in the box, but he works hard.

norwegian: Jeg fikk kalde føtter rett før presentasjonen.
english: I got cold feet right before the presentation.

norwegian: Han gikk rundt grøten i stedet for å si det direkte.
english: He beat around the bush instead of saying it straight.

norwegian: Jeg har svaret på tungespissen, men jeg husker det ikke helt.
english: I have the answer on the tip of my tongue, but I can't quite remember it.

norwegian: Vi snakker alltid rett fra levra med hverandre.
english: We always speak frankly with each other.

norwegian: Jeg er ikke i mitt ess i dag — jeg sov dårlig.
english: I'm not at my best today — I slept badly.

norwegian: Det er jo ikke så vanskelig som du tror.
english: It's not that difficult, as you should already know.

norwegian: Du kommer vel på festen i morgen?
english: You are coming to the party tomorrow, aren't you?

norwegian: Jeg er egentlig ikke så glad i fisk.
english: To be honest, I'm not really that fond of fish.

norwegian: Forresten, har du sett nøklene mine?
english: By the way, have you seen my keys?

norwegian: Han er nemlig veldig flink til å lage mat.
english: The thing is, he's really good at cooking.

norwegian: Altså, jeg skjønner ikke hva du mener.
english: Look, I don't understand what you mean.

norwegian: Det er nok best å ta bussen dit.
english: It's probably best to take the bus there.

norwegian: Det var visst ingen hjemme da jeg ringte.
english: Apparently nobody was home when I called.

norwegian: Kom da, vi rekker bussen hvis vi skynder oss.
english: Come on then, we'll catch the bus if we hurry.

norwegian: Det er liksom ikke det samme uten deg.
english: It's just not the same without you, you know.

norwegian: Jeg visste jo at det ville bli vanskelig.
english: I knew all along that it would be difficult.

norwegian: Hun er vel ikke sint på meg, er hun?
english: She's not angry with me, is she?

norwegian: Vi kan egentlig møtes litt tidligere hvis du vil.
english: We could actually meet a bit earlier if you like.

norwegian: Bussen var nemlig innstilt på grunn av snøen.
english: You see, the bus was cancelled because of the snow.

norwegian: Forresten, vi har ikke bestilt bord ennå.
english: By the way, we haven't booked a table yet.

norwegian: Det skal nok ordne seg, ikke bekymre deg.
english: It'll probably work out fine, don't worry.

norwegian: Altså, det holder ikke å gjøre halvparten av oppgavene.
english: Look, doing half the tasks just isn't good enough.

norwegian: Slapp av da, det er ikke noe å stresse over.
english: Relax then, there's nothing to stress about.

norwegian: Det er jo litt rart at han ikke svarte.
english: It is a bit strange that he didn't reply, isn't it.

norwegian: Vi drar vel ikke dit med tanke på været?
english: We're presumably not going there given the weather?

norwegian: Det var visst ikke første gang det skjedde.
english: Apparently it wasn't the first time that happened.
